app.title: CES-D Depression Screening
app.description: A confidential screening tool for depressive symptoms

disclaimer.title: Important Disclaimer
disclaimer.text: This screening tool is NOT a diagnostic instrument. It is designed to help you identify symptoms of depression and encourage conversation with a healthcare provider. This tool does NOT replace professional mental health care. If you are experiencing a mental health emergency or having thoughts of self-harm, please contact emergency services (911 in the US) or call/text 988 for the Suicide & Crisis Lifeline immediately.
disclaimer.agree: I understand and agree to continue
disclaimer.notDiagnostic: This is a screening tool, NOT a diagnostic instrument. Results should be discussed with a qualified healthcare provider.
disclaimer.notReplacement: This app is NOT a replacement for professional mental health care.
disclaimer.emergency: If you are in crisis, please contact emergency services or call/text 988 (US) immediately.

intro.title: Welcome to the CES-D Screening
intro.description: The Center for Epidemiologic Studies Depression Scale (CES-D) is a 20-question self-report assessment that measures depressive symptoms in the general population. Please answer each question based on how you have felt DURING THE PAST WEEK.
intro.instructions: For each question, select the option that best describes how often you felt or behaved this way during the past week.

questions.q1: I was bothered by things that usually don't bother me.
questions.q2: I did not feel like eating; my appetite was poor.
questions.q3: I felt that I could not shake off the blues even with help from my family or friends.
questions.q4: I felt that I was just as good as other people.
questions.q5: I had trouble keeping my mind on what I was doing.
questions.q7: I felt that everything I did was an effort.
questions.q8: I felt hopeful about the future.
questions.q9: I thought my life had been a failure.
questions.q19: I felt that people dislike me.

responses.r0: Rarely or none of the time (less than 1 day)
responses.r1: Some or a little of the time (1-2 days)
responses.r2: Moderately or much of the time (3-4 days)
responses.r3: Most or all of the time (5-7 days)

minimal.title: Minimal Depressive Symptoms
minimal.description: Your responses suggest minimal depressive symptoms. This indicates that you are generally functioning well without significant signs of depression.
minimal.interpretation: A score below 16 typically indicates that depressive symptoms are not significantly interfering with your daily life. This is a positive result, but continue to monitor your mental health as part of overall wellness.
minimal.recommendations: Maintain healthy habits that support mental well-being: regular exercise, adequate sleep, social connection, and stress management. If you notice changes in how you're feeling, don't hesitate to reach out to a healthcare provider.

mild.title: Mild Depressive Symptoms
mild.description: Your responses suggest mild depressive symptoms. You may be experiencing some difficulties that are affecting your daily life.
mild.interpretation: A score between 16-20 indicates the presence of mild depressive symptoms that may warrant attention. While not severe, these symptoms can affect your quality of life and should not be ignored.
mild.recommendations: Consider speaking with a healthcare provider about your symptoms. They can help determine if further evaluation or support is needed. Self-care activities like regular exercise, maintaining a routine, and connecting with others may be beneficial. This screening is not a diagnosis—professional evaluation is important.

moderate.title: Moderate Depressive Symptoms
moderate.description: Your responses suggest moderate depressive symptoms. You may be experiencing significant difficulties that are affecting your daily life.
moderate.interpretation: A score between 21-25 indicates moderate depressive symptoms that likely interfere with daily functioning. This level of symptoms suggests the need for professional evaluation and support.
moderate.recommendations: We strongly encourage you to speak with a healthcare provider about these symptoms. A mental health professional can provide a proper evaluation and discuss treatment options. Please don't face this alone—support is available and effective treatments exist.

severe.title: Severe Depressive Symptoms
severe.description: Your responses suggest severe depressive symptoms. You may be experiencing significant difficulties that greatly impact your daily life.
severe.interpretation: A score of 26 or above indicates severe depressive symptoms. This level suggests a need for immediate professional attention and support.
severe.recommendations: Please consider speaking with a healthcare provider or mental health professional as soon as possible. Effective treatments are available, and you don't have to face this alone. If you are having thoughts of self-harm, please contact emergency services or a crisis hotline immediately.
severe.urgent: If you are in crisis, please reach out for help right away.

crisis.title: Crisis Resources
crisis.description: If you are experiencing a mental health crisis or having thoughts of self-harm, please reach out immediately:
crisis.crisisTextLine: Crisis Text Line: Text HOME to 741741
crisis.international: International crisis resources available at: findahelpline.com

professional.title: Seeking Professional Help
professional.description: Speaking with a healthcare provider is an important step. Consider:
professional.primaryCare: Schedule an appointment with your primary care physician
professional.mentalHealth: Contact a mental health professional (psychologist, psychiatrist, or therapist)
professional.insurance: Check your insurance provider directory for covered mental health providers
professional.community: Look into community mental health centers or clinics

selfHelp.title: Self-Help Strategies
selfHelp.description: While not a replacement for professional care, these strategies may help support your mental well-being:
selfHelp.exercise: Regular physical activity (even short walks can help)
selfHelp.social: Stay connected with supportive friends and family
selfHelp.routine: Keep a daily routine, even on difficult days
selfHelp.mindfulness: Practice relaxation techniques like deep breathing or mindfulness
selfHelp.journaling: Consider journaling to process thoughts and feelings
selfHelp.limitAlcohol: Limit alcohol and avoid substance use

education.title: Understanding Depression
education.description: Depression is a common and treatable medical condition. It is not a sign of weakness.
education.common: Depression affects millions of people each year
education.treatable: Effective treatments including therapy and medication are available
education.recovery: With proper treatment, people do recover and lead fulfilling lives

privacy.title: Your Privacy
privacy.description: This screening tool is completely private. No data is stored on this device or transmitted anywhere. Your answers exist only in your current browser session. When you close this window, all information is permanently deleted.
